message:
test(cli-robot,bd-s0cmk): pin degraded-mode contract when lexical dir is wiped but DB intact

Bead coding_agent_session_search-s0cmk (child of ibuuh.10). Adds
tests/cli_robot.rs::search_with_intact_db_but_wiped_lexical_degrades_with_truthful_warning
to pin the truthful-fallback surface that `cass search` presents when
the canonical DB is intact but the versioned Tantivy (lexical) tree has
been wiped — an upgrade accident, manual rm, or partial-copy condition.

The existing `search_missing_index_*` tests all use an empty TempDir,
so they only hit the "nothing exists" closure branch in
src/lib.rs's `.ok_or_else(...)` fallback. They never build a DB and
then remove its lexical siblings, which is the real-world half-torn
state this test exercises.

Discovery during test authoring: the contract I initially expected
(exit 3 with kind="missing-index") does not fire on this path.
SearchClient::open_with_options returns Some(client) with
has_tantivy()==false, which takes the degraded-mode branch at
src/lib.rs ~7798. The actual contract pinned here is:

  - exit code 0 (degraded, not hard-fail)
  - stderr carries "Tantivy search index not found" + the exact
    missing path + the recovery command `cass index --full`
  - stdout is still valid JSON so agents can parse it

This is the "fail open even when lexical itself is missing" slice of
ibuuh.10's core-search-contract AC matrix — cass preserves partial
functionality AND surfaces a truthful recovery hint instead of
panicking or lying. If a future refactor flips this to a hard failure,
drops the warning, or stops naming `cass index --full` in the recovery
text, this regression fires immediately alongside the src edit.

Drive-by: rustfmt cleanup on tests/e2e_lexical_fail_open.rs that the
linter introduced after the bd-2hh1s commit landed.

Verified locally:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_cass_p3 \
  rch exec -- cargo test --test cli_robot \
    search_with_intact_db_but_wiped_lexical_degrades_with_truthful_warning
  -> 1 passed in 1.72s

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1104,7 +1104,7 @@
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ncbby","title":"Remove canonical() identity no-op on AnalyticsView enum","description":"## What

Remove the no-op canonical() method on the AnalyticsView enum in src/ui/app.rs and its single call site.

## Current State

Definition (lines 685-688):
```rust
/// Canonicalize legacy views that should no longer be shown in normal UX.
fn canonical(self) -> Self {
    self
}
```

Single call site (line 19462):
```rust
CassMsg::AnalyticsViewChanged(view) => {
    let view = view.canonical();  // <-- this line
    let previous_view = self.analytics_view;
```

## AnalyticsView Enum

7 active variants: Dashboard, Explorer, Heatmap, Breakdowns, Tools, Plans, Coverage. None are legacy. There is nothing to canonicalize.

## Fix — Exactly 2 edits

### Edit 1: Delete lines 685-688
Remove the canonical() method definition from the AnalyticsView impl block.

### Edit 2: Delete line 19462
Remove \`let view = view.canonical();\`. The match arm's pattern variable \`view\` from \`CassMsg::AnalyticsViewChanged(view)\` is used directly by the remaining code. No rebinding needed.

Before:
```rust
CassMsg::AnalyticsViewChanged(view) => {
    let view = view.canonical();
    let previous_view = self.analytics_view;
```

After:
```rust
CassMsg::AnalyticsViewChanged(view) => {
    let previous_view = self.analytics_view;
```

## Existing Test Coverage

Two existing tests already exercise the AnalyticsViewChanged code path:
- \`analytics_view_changed_updates_subview\` (line 32880) — tests view transition with Heatmap
- \`analytics_view_change_resets_selection\` (line 38874) — tests selection reset with Tools

These tests will continue to pass after removing canonical() since it was an identity function.

## Testing

Add a comprehensive all-variants test using \`CassApp::default()\` (the standard test construction pattern in app.rs):

```rust
#[test]
fn analytics_view_changed_applies_all_variants_directly() {
    // canonical() was removed because it was an identity function (returned
    // self unchanged). All 7 AnalyticsView variants are active — none need
    // mapping to a different variant. This test covers all 7 to prevent
    // regressions if a future variant incorrectly bypasses the handler.
    let views = [
        AnalyticsView::Dashboard,
        AnalyticsView::Explorer,
        AnalyticsView::Heatmap,
        AnalyticsView::Breakdowns,
        AnalyticsView::Tools,
        AnalyticsView::Plans,
        AnalyticsView::Coverage,
    ];
    for &target in &views {
        let mut app = CassApp::default();
        let _ = app.update(CassMsg::AnalyticsViewChanged(target));
        assert_eq!(
            app.analytics_view, target,
            "{:?} should be applied directly without canonicalization",
            target
        );
    }
}
```

## Verification

- grep -n \`\\.canonical()\` src/ui/app.rs returns only unrelated string literals (line 44563)
- grep -n \`fn canonical\` src/ui/app.rs returns zero matches
- cargo check --all-targets passes
- cargo test analytics_view -- --nocapture passes (existing + new tests)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:17:26.338388137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:27:11.630794923Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:27:11.630492156Z","close_reason":"Already completed by concurrent agent: canonical() method removed, call site at AnalyticsViewChanged handler updated, analytics_view_changed_applies_all_variants_directly test added at line 32899. Verified: grep returns 0 matches for fn canonical or .canonical().","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cleanup","tui"]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-nd6pc","title":"CRITICAL: export-html leaks proprietary skill content by default","description":"## Severity: CRITICAL — Data Leak

When Claude Code loads a skill via the Skill tool, the FULL skill content is injected into the conversation as a user message (with role 'user'). `cass export-html` then faithfully exports this content into the HTML output. This means:

- **Every proprietary skill's complete source code is included in exports by default**
- Users sharing session transcripts unknowingly leak their entire skill library
- Skills are often highly proprietary, representing significant IP investment
- There is NO warning that skills are being included
- There is NO way to exclude them without manually scrubbing the HTML after export

## Real-World Impact

This was discovered when a user exported a session and published it to GitHub Pages. The export contained the full body of 4 proprietary skills (installer-workmanship, sw/writing-skills, de-slopify, sc). Required emergency git history rewriting to remediate.

## Root Cause

`lib.rs:12346-12401`: The message filter includes ALL user-role messages. Skill injections arrive as user messages with content like:

```
Base directory for this skill: /home/ubuntu/.claude/skills/skill-name

[full SKILL.md content here, potentially thousands of tokens of proprietary IP]
```

The export has no awareness that these are skill injections vs. human-typed messages.

## Required Fix

### 1. Default: Strip skill content from exports

Detect skill injection messages by matching the pattern:
```
Base directory for this skill: <path>
```

When detected, replace the full content with a placeholder:
```
[Skill loaded: skill-name]
```

### 2. Opt-in flag to include skills

```
cass export-html session.jsonl --include-skills
```

Only with this explicit flag should skill content be preserved in the export.

### 3. Also strip system-reminder blocks

The `<system-reminder>` blocks injected by hooks often contain skill listings, hook configuration details, and other metadata that shouldn't be in public exports. These should also be stripped by default with an opt-in `--include-system-reminders` flag.

### Detection Heuristics

Skill injections can be identified by:
1. User-role message starting with `Base directory for this skill:`
2. Content containing YAML frontmatter (`---\nname:\ndescription:\n---`)
3. Content matching `/home/*/\.claude/skills/` or `/home/*/\.codex/skills/` paths

System reminders can be identified by:
1. Content wrapped in `<system-reminder>...</system-reminder>` tags
2. Content containing `The following skills are available for use with the Skill tool:`
3. Content containing `skillInjection:` JSON metadata

## Files

- `src/lib.rs:12346-12401` (message filtering — add skill/system-reminder detection here)
- `src/lib.rs:531` (add `--include-skills` and `--include-system-reminders` CLI flags)
- `src/html_export/template.rs` (render placeholder for stripped skills)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-18T21:33:18.189257941Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T22:34:18.867059003Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T22:34:18.866260538Z","close_reason":"Fixed: both export and export-html drop entire skill messages by default (return None / retain false)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-nfk","title":"Phase 3: CLI & Robot Output Provenance Integration","description":"# Phase 3: CLI & Robot Output Provenance Integration

## Overview
This phase extends the CLI interface and robot-docs output format to expose provenance
information to users and AI agents. After Phase 2 populates provenance in storage,
Phase 3 makes it queryable and visible.

## Goals
1. Add `--source` filter flag to search/timeline commands
2. Extend SearchHit and TimelineEntry structs with source metadata
3. Update robot-docs output to include provenance in machine-readable format
4. Ensure backward compatibility with existing CLI workflows

## Context
The robot-docs format is crucial for AI agent consumption. Adding provenance enables
agents to distinguish between local and remote sessions, filter by machine, and
understand the origin of conversation data.

## Dependencies
- Requires Phase 2 completion (provenance stored in SQLite + Tantivy)
- coding_agent_session_search-bfk (Phase 2 epic) must be complete

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cass search --source=laptop-hostname "query"` filters to that source only
- [ ] `cass search --source=remote "query"` filters to all non-local sources
- [ ] `cass search --source=local "query"` filters to local-only
- [ ] Robot output includes source_hostname, source_type, sync_timestamp fields
- [ ] All existing tests pass (backward compatible)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T06:00:53.630278Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T18:04:46.618371Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T18:04:46.618371Z","close_reason":"Phase 3 complete: All CLI provenance features implemented - P3.1 search --source, P3.2 timeline --source, P3.3 SearchHit provenance, P3.4 robot-docs provenance, P3.5 timeline provenance, P3.6 schema updates, P3.7 stats --source/--by-source","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-nfk","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-bfk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ngou","title":"[P2] Opt 6: Streaming Canonicalization (Reduce String Allocations)","description":"# Optimization 6: Streaming Canonicalization

## Problem Statement

The `canonicalize_for_embedding` function shows as a hotspot in indexing benchmarks:

### Benchmark Evidence
```
canonicalize_long_message: 951 µs
```

Nearly 1ms per long message is significant when indexing thousands of messages.

### Current Implementation (canonicalize.rs:80-95)
```rust
pub fn canonicalize_for_embedding(text: &str) -> String {
    let normalized: String = text.nfc().collect();  // Allocation #1
    let stripped = strip_markdown_and_code(&normalized);  // Allocation #2
    let whitespace_normalized = normalize_whitespace(&stripped);  // Allocation #3
    let filtered = filter_low_signal(&whitespace_normalized);  // Allocation #4
    truncate_to_chars(&filtered, MAX_EMBED_CHARS)  // Allocation #5
}
```

**Analysis**: 4-5 full String allocations per call. Each allocation:
- Allocates new heap memory
- Copies all characters
- Deallocates previous string

## Proposed Solution

Single-pass processing with buffer reuse, eliminating intermediate String allocations.

### Implementation Location
- File: `src/search/canonicalize.rs`
- Add new function: `canonicalize_for_embedding_streaming`

### Code Sketch
```rust
pub fn canonicalize_for_embedding_streaming(text: &str) -> String {
    // Pre-allocate result buffer (avoid multiple reallocations)
    let mut result = String::with_capacity(text.len().min(MAX_EMBED_CHARS + 100));
    
    // NFC normalization requires full collection (look-ahead for combining chars)
    // This allocation is unavoidable
    let normalized: String = text.nfc().collect();

    let mut in_code_block = false;
    let mut pending_space = false;
    let mut char_count = 0;

    for line in normalized.lines() {
        // Handle code block markers
        if line.starts_with("```") {
            in_code_block = !in_code_block;
            continue;
        }
        
        // Skip code blocks and low-signal content
        if in_code_block || is_low_signal_line(line) {
            continue;
        }

        // Process line character by character
        for ch in strip_markdown_inline(line).chars() {
            if ch.is_whitespace() {
                pending_space = true;
            } else {
                if pending_space && !result.is_empty() {
                    result.push(' ');
                    char_count += 1;
                }
                pending_space = false;
                result.push(ch);
                char_count += 1;
            }
            
            if char_count >= MAX_EMBED_CHARS {
                return result;
            }
        }
        pending_space = true; // Space between lines
    }

    result
}

#[inline]
fn is_low_signal_line(line: &str) -> bool {
    let trimmed = line.trim();
    trimmed.is_empty() 
        || trimmed.starts_with("//")
        || trimmed.starts_with('#')
        || trimmed.starts_with("---")
        || trimmed.chars().all(|c| !c.is_alphanumeric())
}

fn strip_markdown_inline(line: &str) -> impl Iterator<Item = char> + '_ {
    // Strip inline markdown: **, *, `, [], etc.
    // Returns iterator, no allocation
    line.chars().filter(|c| !matches!(c, '*' | '`' | '[' | ']' | '#'))
}
```

## Allocation Analysis

### Before (Current)
| Step | Allocation |
|------|------------|
| NFC normalize | Full string |
| strip_markdown_and_code | Full string |
| normalize_whitespace | Full string |
| filter_low_signal | Full string |
| truncate_to_chars | Full string |
| **Total** | **5 allocations** |

### After (Streaming)
| Step | Allocation |
|------|------------|
| NFC normalize | Full string (unavoidable) |
| Result buffer | Single pre-sized allocation |
| **Total** | **2 allocations** |

## Why NFC Normalization Can't Be Streamed

Unicode NFC (Canonical Decomposition, followed by Canonical Composition) requires look-ahead for combining characters. For example:
- `é` (U+00E9) = precomposed
- `e` + `́` (U+0065 + U+0301) = decomposed

NFC must see both codepoints before deciding on output. This requires buffering the entire string.

**Mitigation**: NFC is typically cheap (~100-200µs for long messages). The savings come from eliminating the other 3-4 allocations.

## Expected Impact

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| `canonicalize_long_message` | 951 µs | ~300 µs |
| Allocations per call | 5 | 2 |
| Index-time impact | Noticeable | Reduced |

**Note**: This only affects index-time, not query-time. Lexical search doesn't use canonicalization.

## Impact on Semantic Search Query Path

The query embedding path also calls `canonicalize_for_embedding` on the query text. Queries are typically short, so the impact is minimal. But the optimization applies equally.

## Isomorphism Proof

The streaming version must produce byte-for-byte identical output:
1. **Same NFC normalization**: Same input → same NFC output
2. **Same markdown stripping**: Same rules, different implementation
3. **Same whitespace normalization**: Collapse runs, preserve word boundaries
4. **Same low-signal filtering**: Same heuristics
5. **Same truncation**: Same MAX_EMBED_CHARS limit

### Verification
```rust
#[test]
fn streaming_matches_original() {
    for text in test_corpus() {
        let original = canonicalize_for_embedding(text);
        let streaming = canonicalize_for_embedding_streaming(text);
        assert_eq!(original, streaming, "Mismatch for: {:?}", text);
    }
}
```

## Verification Plan

1. **Equivalence test**: Original vs streaming produce identical output for test corpus
2. **content_hash test**: Hash of canonicalized output matches
3. **Benchmark**: Measure allocation reduction with criterion
4. **Property-based test**: Fuzz with arbitrary Unicode strings

## Rollback Strategy

Environment variable `CASS_STREAMING_CANONICALIZE=0` to:
- Use original multi-allocation implementation
- Useful for debugging canonicalization issues

## Dependencies

- None (index-time optimization, independent of search path)
- Can be implemented in parallel with vector search optimizations","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-10T03:02:10.211219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T03:40:06.524555Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T03:40:06.524555Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of 5p55 - consolidated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-nkc9","title":"[Task] Opt 8.4: Benchmark streaming indexing memory","description":"## Objective
Benchmark memory usage improvement from streaming indexing.

## Benchmark Scenarios

### 1. Peak RSS Comparison
```bash
# Batch mode
CASS_STREAMING_INDEX=0 /usr/bin/time -v cargo run -- index --full 2>&1 | grep "Maximum resident"

# Streaming mode  
/usr/bin/time -v cargo run -- index --full 2>&1 | grep "Maximum resident"
```

### 2. Memory Timeline
Use `heaptrack` or `valgrind --tool=massif`:
- Plot memory usage over time
- Identify peak and steady-state differences
- Measure allocation rate

### 3. Throughput Comparison
```rust
#[bench]
fn bench_index_batch(b: &mut Bencher) {
    std::env::set_var("CASS_STREAMING_INDEX", "0");
    let corpus = generate_corpus(1000);
    b.iter(|| index_corpus(&corpus))
}

#[bench]
fn bench_index_streaming(b: &mut Bencher) {
    std::env::remove_var("CASS_STREAMING_INDEX");
    let corpus = generate_corpus(1000);
    b.iter(|| index_corpus(&corpus))
}
```

### 4. Channel Overhead Profiling
- Measure time spent in channel operations
- Compare with batch collection time
- Identify optimal buffer size

## Success Criteria
- Peak RSS: 295 MB → ~150 MB (50% reduction)
- Throughput: No more than 10% regression
- Memory timeline: Flat vs spikey profile

## Documentation
- Before/after memory profiles
- Channel sizing recommendations
- Trade-off analysis

## Parent Feature
coding_agent_session_search-ug6i (Opt 8: Streaming Backpressure)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T03:28:56.348470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T02:38:44.775274Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T02:38:44.775214Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: sql_placeholders() in query.rs:130 with pre-sized capacity, run_streaming_index() in indexer/mod.rs:344 with bounded channel backpressure","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-nkc9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-decq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-nkyq","title":"P1.4a: Verify Command for CI Pipelines","description":"# P1.4a: Verify Command for CI Pipelines

## Goal
Provide `cass pages --verify` to validate an existing export bundle for CI/CD. The verifier must confirm correct structure, config schema, payload integrity, and the absence of secrets in site/.

## CLI Interface

```
cass pages --verify <PATH>

OPTIONS:
  --json         Output machine-readable JSON
  -v, --verbose  Include detailed check results
```

`PATH` may be either the export root (containing site/) or the site/ directory itself. Verify resolves to the site/ directory.

## Verification Checks

### 1) Required Files
- index.html
- config.json
- sw.js
- viewer.js
- auth.js
- styles.css
- robots.txt
- .nojekyll
- payload/ (with chunk files)
- integrity.json (if present, must validate)

### 2) config.json Schema
Validate required fields and types:
- version, export_id (base64, 16 bytes), base_nonce (base64, 12 bytes)
- algorithm == aes-256-gcm
- compression in {deflate, zstd, none}
- payload.chunk_size <= 32 MiB (default 8 MiB), payload.chunk_count > 0
- payload.files list length == chunk_count
- key_slots[] with slot_type, kdf, salt, nonce, wrapped_dek
- no human labels or PII fields in public config

### 3) Payload Manifest and Size Limits
- payload/chunk-00000.bin ... payload/chunk-N.bin exist and are contiguous
- each chunk file <= 100 MB (GitHub Pages hard limit)
- total site size computed and reported

### 4) integrity.json (if present)
- recompute sha256 for each site/ file
- compare with integrity.json entries
- fail on mismatch; report missing/extra files

### 5) Secret Leakage Checks
- ensure site/ does not contain recovery-secret.txt, qr-code.*, master-key.json, or private/
- ensure config.json does not include secret material (no plaintext passwords, no labels)

### 6) Optional blobs/
- if blobs/ exists, each filename must be sha256-*.bin
- blobs are included in integrity.json

## JSON Output (example)
```json
{
  "status": "valid",
  "checks": {
    "required_files": true,
    "config_schema": true,
    "payload_manifest": true,
    "size_limits": true,
    "integrity": true,
    "no_secrets_in_site": true
  },
  "warnings": [],
  "site_size_bytes": 25678901
}
```

## Test Requirements

### Unit Tests
- Missing required files -> failure
- Invalid config.json fields -> failure
- Chunk count mismatch -> failure
- integrity.json mismatch -> failure

### Integration Tests
- Verify a known-good fixture export passes
- Verify a fixture with private files in site/ fails

### E2E Script
- Build export -> bundle -> cass pages --verify --json
- Log each check clearly with PASS/FAIL and timing

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/pages/verify.rs
- src/cli/pages.rs (add --verify)
- tests/pages_verify.rs
- tests/fixtures/pages_verify/

## Exit Criteria
1. Verify passes on valid bundles and fails on invalid ones
2. JSON output is stable for CI parsing
3. Clear error messages in verbose mode","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T05:01:53.627990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T00:47:55.479677Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T00:47:55.479677Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of coding_agent_session_search-euch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-nkyq","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-km9j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Job fails on run_all.sh non-zero exit","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T05:49:24.342706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:43:41.691825Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:43:41.691739Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-rzit","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2128","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-rzit","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3eb7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-rzrv","title":"RRF hybrid fusion algorithm","description":"## Purpose
Implement Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) for combining lexical and semantic results.

## RRF Formula
score(d) = Σ 1/(k + rank(d)) where k=60 (industry standard)

## Implementation
```rust
const RRF_K: f32 = 60.0;

pub fn rrf_fuse(
    lexical: &[SearchHit],
    semantic: &[VectorSearchResult],
    limit: usize,
) -> Vec<HybridSearchHit> {
    let mut scores: HashMap<u64, HybridScore> = HashMap::new();

    for (rank, hit) in lexical.iter().enumerate() {
        let entry = scores.entry(hit.message_id).or_default();
        entry.rrf += 1.0 / (RRF_K + rank as f32 + 1.0);
        entry.lexical_rank = Some(rank);
        entry.lexical_score = Some(hit.bm25_score);
    }

    for (rank, hit) in semantic.iter().enumerate() {
        let entry = scores.entry(hit.message_id).or_default();
        entry.rrf += 1.0 / (RRF_K + rank as f32 + 1.0);
        entry.semantic_rank = Some(rank);
        entry.semantic_score = Some(hit.similarity);
    }

    // Sort by RRF descending, then apply tie-breaking
    let mut results: Vec<_> = scores.into_iter().collect();
    results.sort_by(|a, b| {
        // Primary: RRF score descending
        match b.1.rrf.partial_cmp(&a.1.rrf) {
            Some(Ordering::Equal) | None => {
                // Tie-break 1: Prefer documents in both lists
                let a_both = a.1.lexical_rank.is_some() && a.1.semantic_rank.is_some();
                let b_both = b.1.lexical_rank.is_some() && b.1.semantic_rank.is_some();
                match (b_both, a_both) {
                    (true, false) => Ordering::Greater,
                    (false, true) => Ordering::Less,
                    _ => {
                        // Tie-break 2: By MessageID for determinism
                        a.0.cmp(&b.0)
                    }
                }
            }
            Some(ord) => ord,
        }
    });
    // ...
}
```

## Tie-Breaking Rules (Critical for Determinism)
When RRF scores are identical:
1. Prefer documents appearing in BOTH lexical and semantic results
2. Fall back to MessageID ascending for deterministic ordering

Without explicit tie-breaking, results could vary between runs, causing confusion.

## Candidate Depth
Fetch 3× limit from each source for better fusion quality.

## Why RRF?
- No score normalization needed (uses ranks, not scores)
- Robust across query types without tuning
- Simple: one parameter (k=60)
- Industry standard: Elasticsearch, Qdrant, Azure AI Search

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Documents in both lists get higher scores
- [ ] Rankings are DETERMINISTIC (tie-breaking works)
- [ ] Handles disjoint result sets
- [ ] Performance: <5ms for 500 candidates
- [ ] Test: Same query always produces same order

## Depends On
- hyb.search (Semantic search)

## References
- Plan: Section 6 (Hybrid Search with RRF)","notes":"Implemented RRF fusion + hybrid search wiring (search_hybrid, rrf_fuse_hits) and updated Hybrid status message. cargo check/clippy ok; cargo fmt --check fails due to pre-existing formatting diffs across many files.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T01:25:19.840318Z","updated_at":"2026-01-05T22:59:36.441409Z","closed_at":"2025-12-19T20:05:19.465426Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-rzrv","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9vjh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-rzst","title":"P4.1a: Bundle Builder & Static Site Generator","description":"# P4.1a: Bundle Builder & Static Site Generator

## Goal
Build the deployable static site bundle (site/) plus the private offline artifacts (private/) from an encrypted export. Output must match the chunked AEAD format and be safe for public hosting (GitHub Pages / Cloudflare Pages) with no secrets in site/.

## Output Structure (Split Output)

```
cass-pages-export/
├── site/                       # DEPLOY THIS (public)
│   ├── index.html              # Auth UI + app shell (no inline scripts)
│   ├── .nojekyll               # Disable Jekyll
│   ├── robots.txt              # Disallow crawling
│   ├── config.json             # Public params + payload manifest (no secrets)
│   ├── integrity.json          # sha256 + size for each public file
│   ├── payload/                # Chunked AEAD ciphertext (ALWAYS used)
│   │   ├── chunk-00000.bin
│   │   ├── chunk-00001.bin
│   │   └── ...
│   ├── blobs/                  # Optional encrypted attachment blobs
│   │   ├── sha256-abc123.bin
│   │   └── ...
│   ├── sw.js                   # COOP/COEP service worker
│   ├── viewer.js               # Main app logic
│   ├── auth.js                 # Auth flow
│   ├── search.js               # Search UI
│   ├── conversation.js         # Conversation rendering
│   ├── styles.css              # Tailwind-based styles
│   ├── vendor/
│   │   ├── sqlite3.js
│   │   ├── sqlite3.wasm
│   │   ├── sqlite3-opfs.js
│   │   ├── argon2-wasm.js
│   │   ├── argon2-wasm.wasm
│   │   ├── fflate.min.js
│   │   ├── marked.min.js
│   │   ├── prism.min.js
│   │   ├── dompurify.min.js
│   │   └── html5-qrcode.min.js
│   ├── assets/
│   │   ├── logo.svg
│   │   └── icons.svg
│   └── README.md               # Public archive description (no secrets)
└── private/                    # NEVER DEPLOY (offline storage only)
    ├── recovery-secret.txt
    ├── qr-code.png
    ├── qr-code.svg
    ├── integrity-fingerprint.txt
    └── master-key.json         # Optional encrypted DEK backup
```

Notes:
- No archive.enc or encrypted.bin. All exports use chunked AEAD in payload/.
- config.json is public and contains only parameters (no secrets or labels).
- index.html must not inline config (CSP stays strict). Config is fetched.
- integrity.json is public; integrity-fingerprint.txt is a short summary hash for out-of-band verification.

## Builder Responsibilities

1. Create site/ and private/ directories.
2. Write config.json from encryption pipeline (version, export_id, base_nonce, compression, kdf defaults, payload manifest, key slots, exported_at, cass_version).
3. Write payload/chunk-*.bin files and optional blobs/ for attachments.
4. Copy web assets (HTML, JS, CSS, vendor, assets) from web/dist or pages_assets.
5. Generate integrity.json for all files in site/ and a private integrity-fingerprint.txt.
6. Write robots.txt and .nojekyll.
7. Write README.md (public) and private recovery artifacts.
8. Optional: generate PWA manifest if enabled.

## Security / CSP Requirements

- index.html uses CSP meta tag (no inline scripts, only 'self' and 'wasm-unsafe-eval').
- No secrets in site/ (no recovery secret, no QR images, no master-key backup).
- No inline config. All config is read from config.json via fetch.

## Test Requirements

### Unit Tests
- config.json schema round-trip
- integrity.json includes all public files, correct hashes and sizes
- payload manifest count and file names match generated chunks

### Integration Tests
- Build a small export and assert exact directory tree
- Verify site/ has no private artifacts
- Verify optional blobs/ is included only when attachments enabled

### E2E Script
- Build export -> bundle -> cass pages --verify
- Optional: start preview server and load auth page
- Log each check with clear PASS/FAIL markers

## Files to Create/Modify

- src/pages/bundle.rs
- src/pages/integrity.rs
- src/pages/assets.rs (copy logic)
- tests/pages_bundle.rs
- tests/fixtures/pages_bundle/

## Exit Criteria

1. site/ deploys cleanly on GitHub Pages and Cloudflare Pages
2. All required files present and integrity.json validates
3. No secrets in site/
4. config.json matches encryption output
5. Chunked payload loads in web viewer","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T03:34:00.885932Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T17:04:34.420767Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T17:04:34.420767Z","close_reason":"Implemented BundleBuilder with full functionality: creates site/ and private/ directories, copies encrypted payload, embeds web assets, generates integrity.json with SHA256 hashes, writes robots.txt/.nojekyll, writes private recovery artifacts, integrated with wizard.  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-sdoxg","title":"Replace FNV-1a byte-loop stable_content_hash with xxhash or gxhash in search/query.rs","description":"FILE: src/search/query.rs (lines 1404-1453)

CURRENT COST:
`stable_content_hash` and its helper `hash_bytes` implement FNV-1a by byte, in a hand-rolled scalar loop:

```rust
fn hash_bytes(mut hash: u64, bytes: &[u8]) -> u64 {
    const FNV_PRIME: u64 = 1099511628211;
    for byte in bytes {
        hash ^= u64::from(*byte);
        hash = hash.wrapping_mul(FNV_PRIME);
    }
    hash
}
```

`stable_content_hash` is called on dedup/hash paths per hit (FusedHit construction, dedup key computation, content-hash-based dedup in `resolve_semantic_doc_ids_for_hits_distinguishes_same_source_path_line_by_content_hash`, etc.) — for search results with content up to 2000 chars this is a 2KB byte-by-byte mul chain with no SIMD, no vectorization, and sequential data dependencies. Modern machines can hash >10 GB/s with xxhash3 or gxhash vs FNV-1a at ~1 GB/s.

PROPOSED CHANGE:
Replace the body with `twox_hash::XxHash64` or the `gxhash` crate (both already transitive deps via frankensearch — verify with `cargo tree | grep -E 'xxhash|gxhash'`). Preserve the whitespace-invariant split semantics (iterate over split_whitespace tokens and feed them through the hasher with a space separator between tokens). Use a fixed seed (0) for deterministic cross-run output. The function is NOT on-disk format — grep confirms it feeds in-memory dedup HashMap keys and tests, so changing the hash value is safe (no schema migration).

EXPECTED WIN:
4-10x faster hashing for long content bodies (~1-2 KB typical tool outputs). Each search with 100 hits computes ~100 hashes, so the saved µs add up. Also used by stable_hit_hash which is even hotter.

VERIFICATION:
1. Before changing, run the full dedup test suite: `cargo test --lib -- resolve_semantic_doc_ids_for_hits_distinguishes`
2. After change, all of `cargo test --lib search::query::` must pass (includes the ~20 content_hash-consuming tests around lines 7835-11931).
3. Benchmark: add a criterion bench for `stable_content_hash` with 200/2000 char inputs if one doesn't exist, or measure via `search_perf::rrf_fusion_*` which consumes it transitively.
4. Update any test that hardcodes specific hash numeric output (`rg 'stable_content_hash\(' tests/`).

SAFETY NOTE:
Confirm stable_content_hash is not persisted to disk (message_docs / storage) — only used in-memory for dedup. grep src/storage and src/indexer before committing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:46:14.489622644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:00:41.300746599Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:00:41.300380744Z","close_reason":"Replaced FNV-1a byte-loop in stable_content_hash + stable_hit_hash with xxhash_rust::xxh3::Xxh3 (xxh3-64). Tokenization preserved (split_whitespace + 0x20 separator), dedup semantics byte-identical apart from the hash value itself (in-memory only, not persisted). Added xxhash-rust as direct dep with xxh3 feature. Landed in commit 88a647ff. rch cargo check --all-targets: green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hashing","optimization","performance","search"]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-sdx6","title":"E2E Test Runner Script with Detailed Logging","description":"# E2E Test Runner Script with Detailed Logging

## What
Create a comprehensive shell script that runs all cass E2E tests with:
- Detailed, structured logging
- Colored human-readable output
- Timing information
- Phase separation (unit, integration, E2E)
- Failure diagnostics
- Summary report

## Why
Currently tests are run via `cargo test` which provides minimal output. Developers
and CI need:
- Clear visibility into what's being tested
- Timing data for performance regression detection
- Detailed failure context for debugging
- Structured output for CI parsing

## Technical Design

### Main Test Runner Script
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/test-all.sh
# Comprehensive test runner with detailed logging

set -euo pipefail

# =============================================================================
# Configuration
# =============================================================================

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
LOG_DIR="${PROJECT_ROOT}/test-logs"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
LOG_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/test_${TIMESTAMP}.log"

# Colors
RED='\\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\\033[0;36m'
NC='\\033[0m' # No Color

# Verbosity
VERBOSE=${VERBOSE:-0}
PARALLEL=${PARALLEL:-1}
INCLUDE_SSH=${INCLUDE_SSH:-0}
INCLUDE_SLOW=${INCLUDE_SLOW:-0}

# =============================================================================
# Logging Functions
# =============================================================================

log() {
    local level=$1
    shift
    local msg="$*"
    local timestamp=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N")
    
    # Color based on level
    case $level in
        INFO)  color=$GREEN ;;
        WARN)  color=$YELLOW ;;
        ERROR) color=$RED ;;
        DEBUG) color=$CYAN ;;
        *)     color=$NC ;;
    esac
    
    # Console output (colored)
    echo -e "${color}[${timestamp}] [${level}]${NC} ${msg}"
    
    # Log file output (plain)
    echo "[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${msg}" >> "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_section() {
    local title=$1
    echo ""
    log INFO "=================================================================="
    log INFO "  $title"
    log INFO "=================================================================="
}

log_subsection() {
    local title=$1
    echo ""
    log INFO "--- $title ---"
}

# =============================================================================
# Timing Functions
# =============================================================================

declare -A TIMINGS

time_start() {
    local name=$1
    TIMINGS["${name}_start"]=$(date +%s.%N)
}

time_end() {
    local name=$1
    local start=${TIMINGS["${name}_start"]}
    local end=$(date +%s.%N)
    local duration=$(echo "$end - $start" | bc)
    TIMINGS["${name}_duration"]=$duration
    log INFO "⏱  $name completed in ${duration}s"
}

# =============================================================================
# Test Phases
# =============================================================================

run_unit_tests() {
    log_section "PHASE 1: Unit Tests (src/ modules)"
    time_start "unit_tests"
    
    local args=("--lib" "--color=always")
    [[ $PARALLEL -eq 1 ]] && args+=("--jobs" "$(nproc)")
    
    if cargo test "${args[@]}" 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
        log INFO "✓ Unit tests passed"
        UNIT_RESULT=0
    else
        log ERROR "✗ Unit tests failed"
        UNIT_RESULT=1
    fi
    
    time_end "unit_tests"
    return $UNIT_RESULT
}

run_connector_tests() {
    log_section "PHASE 2: Connector Tests (real fixtures)"
    time_start "connector_tests"
    
    local connectors=("claude" "codex" "gemini" "cline" "aider" "amp" "opencode" "pi_agent")
    local failed=0
    
    for conn in "${connectors[@]}"; do
        log_subsection "Testing connector: $conn"
        if cargo test --test "connector_${conn}" --color=always 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
            log INFO "  ✓ $conn connector passed"
        else
            log ERROR "  ✗ $conn connector failed"
            ((failed++))
        fi
    done
    
    time_end "connector_tests"
    
    if [[ $failed -gt 0 ]]; then
        log ERROR "$failed connector test(s) failed"
        return 1
    fi
    log INFO "✓ All connector tests passed"
    return 0
}

run_cli_tests() {
    log_section "PHASE 3: CLI E2E Tests"
    time_start "cli_tests"
    
    local test_files=(
        "e2e_cli_flows"
        "e2e_sources"
        "e2e_search_index"
        "e2e_filters"
        "cli_index"
        "cli_robot"
    )
    local failed=0
    
    for test in "${test_files[@]}"; do
        log_subsection "Running $test"
        if cargo test --test "$test" --color=always 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
            log INFO "  ✓ $test passed"
        else
            log ERROR "  ✗ $test failed"
            ((failed++))
        fi
    done
    
    time_end "cli_tests"
    
    if [[ $failed -gt 0 ]]; then
        log ERROR "$failed CLI test(s) failed"
        return 1
    fi
    log INFO "✓ All CLI tests passed"
    return 0
}

run_ui_tests() {
    log_section "PHASE 4: UI Component Tests"
    time_start "ui_tests"
    
    local test_files=("ui_components" "ui_footer" "ui_help" "ui_hotkeys" "ui_snap")
    local failed=0
    
    for test in "${test_files[@]}"; do
        log_subsection "Running $test"
        if cargo test --test "$test" --color=always 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
            log INFO "  ✓ $test passed"
        else
            log ERROR "  ✗ $test failed"
            ((failed++))
        fi
    done
    
    time_end "ui_tests"
    
    if [[ $failed -gt 0 ]]; then
        log ERROR "$failed UI test(s) failed"
        return 1
    fi
    log INFO "✓ All UI tests passed"
    return 0
}

run_ssh_tests() {
    if [[ $INCLUDE_SSH -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_section "PHASE 5: SSH Tests (SKIPPED - set INCLUDE_SSH=1 to run)"
        return 0
    fi
    
    log_section "PHASE 5: SSH Integration Tests (requires Docker)"
    time_start "ssh_tests"
    
    # Check Docker is available
    if \! command -v docker &> /dev/null; then
        log WARN "Docker not available, skipping SSH tests"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Build SSH test container
    log_subsection "Building SSH test container"
    if \! docker build -t cass-ssh-test -f tests/docker/Dockerfile.sshd tests/docker/ 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
        log ERROR "Failed to build SSH test container"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Run SSH tests
    log_subsection "Running SSH integration tests"
    if cargo test --test ssh_sync_integration -- --ignored --color=always 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
        log INFO "✓ SSH integration tests passed"
        SSH_RESULT=0
    else
        log ERROR "✗ SSH integration tests failed"
        SSH_RESULT=1
    fi
    
    time_end "ssh_tests"
    return $SSH_RESULT
}

run_slow_tests() {
    if [[ $INCLUDE_SLOW -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_section "PHASE 6: Slow Tests (SKIPPED - set INCLUDE_SLOW=1 to run)"
        return 0
    fi
    
    log_section "PHASE 6: Slow/Performance Tests"
    time_start "slow_tests"
    
    local test_files=("watch_e2e" "robot_perf" "concurrent_search")
    local failed=0
    
    for test in "${test_files[@]}"; do
        log_subsection "Running $test"
        if cargo test --test "$test" --color=always 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
            log INFO "  ✓ $test passed"
        else
            log ERROR "  ✗ $test failed"
            ((failed++))
        fi
    done
    
    time_end "slow_tests"
    
    if [[ $failed -gt 0 ]]; then
        log ERROR "$failed slow test(s) failed"
        return 1
    fi
    log INFO "✓ All slow tests passed"
    return 0
}

# =============================================================================
# Summary Report
# =============================================================================

print_summary() {
    log_section "TEST SUMMARY"
    
    echo ""
    printf "%-30s %10s %10s\\n" "Phase" "Duration" "Status"
    printf "%-30s %10s %10s\\n" "-----" "--------" "------"
    
    for key in unit_tests connector_tests cli_tests ui_tests ssh_tests slow_tests; do
        local duration=${TIMINGS["${key}_duration"]:-"N/A"}
        local status
        
        case $key in
            unit_tests)      status=${UNIT_RESULT:-"?"};;
            connector_tests) status=${CONNECTOR_RESULT:-"?"};;
            cli_tests)       status=${CLI_RESULT:-"?"};;
            ui_tests)        status=${UI_RESULT:-"?"};;
            ssh_tests)       status=${SSH_RESULT:-"skipped"};;
            slow_tests)      status=${SLOW_RESULT:-"skipped"};;
        esac
        
        if [[ $status == "0" ]]; then
            printf "%-30s %10s ${GREEN}%10s${NC}\\n" "$key" "${duration}s" "PASS"
        elif [[ $status == "skipped" ]]; then
            printf "%-30s %10s ${YELLOW}%10s${NC}\\n" "$key" "-" "SKIP"
        else
            printf "%-30s %10s ${RED}%10s${NC}\\n" "$key" "${duration}s" "FAIL"
        fi
    done
    
    echo ""
    log INFO "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
}

# =============================================================================
# Main
# =============================================================================

main() {
    mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
    
    log_section "CASS TEST RUNNER"
    log INFO "Project root: $PROJECT_ROOT"
    log INFO "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
    log INFO "Timestamp: $TIMESTAMP"
    log INFO "Settings: PARALLEL=$PARALLEL INCLUDE_SSH=$INCLUDE_SSH INCLUDE_SLOW=$INCLUDE_SLOW"
    
    cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
    
    # Run all phases, collecting results
    run_unit_tests && UNIT_RESULT=0 || UNIT_RESULT=1
    run_connector_tests && CONNECTOR_RESULT=0 || CONNECTOR_RESULT=1
    run_cli_tests && CLI_RESULT=0 || CLI_RESULT=1
    run_ui_tests && UI_RESULT=0 || UI_RESULT=1
    run_ssh_tests && SSH_RESULT=0 || SSH_RESULT=1
    run_slow_tests && SLOW_RESULT=0 || SLOW_RESULT=1
    
    print_summary
    
    # Exit with failure if any required phase failed
    if [[ ${UNIT_RESULT:-1} -ne 0 ]] || \
       [[ ${CONNECTOR_RESULT:-1} -ne 0 ]] || \
       [[ ${CLI_RESULT:-1} -ne 0 ]] || \
       [[ ${UI_RESULT:-1} -ne 0 ]]; then
        log ERROR "TESTS FAILED"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    log INFO "ALL TESTS PASSED"
    exit 0
}

# Parse arguments
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
        -v|--verbose)   VERBOSE=1; shift ;;
        --no-parallel)  PARALLEL=0; shift ;;
        --include-ssh)  INCLUDE_SSH=1; shift ;;
        --include-slow) INCLUDE_SLOW=1; shift ;;
        --all)          INCLUDE_SSH=1; INCLUDE_SLOW=1; shift ;;
        -h|--help)
            echo "Usage: $0 [options]"
            echo "  -v, --verbose     Verbose output"
            echo "  --no-parallel     Run tests sequentially"
            echo "  --include-ssh     Include SSH integration tests (requires Docker)"
            echo "  --include-slow    Include slow/performance tests"
            echo "  --all             Include all optional tests"
            exit 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown option: $1"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

main
```

### JSON Log Format
For CI parsing, also output JSON log:
```bash
# In log() function, also append JSON
echo "{\"ts\":\"${timestamp}\",\"level\":\"${level}\",\"msg\":\"${msg}\"}" >> "${LOG_FILE%.log}.jsonl"
```

### Quick Test Script
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/test-quick.sh
# Fast feedback loop for development

set -euo pipefail

echo "🚀 Running quick tests..."

# Just unit tests and most critical integration tests
cargo test --lib --color=always -- --test-threads=4
cargo test --test connector_claude --color=always
cargo test --test e2e_cli_flows --color=always

echo "✓ Quick tests passed"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] scripts/test-all.sh runs all test phases
- [ ] scripts/test-quick.sh provides fast feedback loop
- [ ] Colored output for terminal
- [ ] Plain text log file created
- [ ] JSON log file for CI parsing
- [ ] Timing information per phase
- [ ] Summary table at end
- [ ] Exit code reflects overall status
- [ ] CI integration (GitHub Actions workflow)

## Usage Examples
```bash
# Full test run
./scripts/test-all.sh

# With SSH tests
./scripts/test-all.sh --include-ssh

# Everything including slow tests
./scripts/test-all.sh --all

# Quick development feedback
./scripts/test-quick.sh
```

## Dependencies
- bash 4.0+ (for associative arrays)
- bc (for timing calculations)
- tee, date (standard utils)
